title: Explaining Culture to Technology, Paul Ford | Compile 26
summary: Paul Ford argues that culture functions as a distributed, lossy prediction model and compares it to an operating system. He explores what technologists can learn from how magazines operate and contends that rhetoric carries greater weight than facts in cultural contexts.
writeup: In this Compile 26 talk, Paul Ford frames culture as a distributed, lossy prediction model and likens it to an operating system. He draws lessons for technologists from how magazines operate, and he argues that rhetoric carries more weight than facts in cultural contexts. The core takeaway: cultural systems run on prediction and persuasion rather than raw accuracy, a framing that reorients how builders might think about technology's relationship to the social world it enters.
